title: Chlorophytum engleri
intro: Chlorophytum engleri, also known as the Spider Plant or Engler's Spider Plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering various health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Chlorophytum engleri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chlorophytum engleri is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of a tea or an infusion.
taxonomy: Chlorophytum engleri belongs to the family Asparagaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as asparagus or lilyturf.
